input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <CollectionView.Header>
            <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                         IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
                <!-- wrapping trick isn't needed; see correct approach below -->
            </RefreshView>
        </CollectionView.Header>

    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

Actually, `RefreshView` should **wrap** the `CollectionView`, not sit inside it:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate>
                <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}"
                           FontSize="16"
                           VerticalOptions="Center" />
                    <Label Grid.Column="1"
                           Text="›"
                           FontSize="20"
                           VerticalOptions="Center" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>

</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel** (using MVVM Community Toolkit):

```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    public ItemsViewModel(IApiService api, INavigationService nav)
    {
        _api = api;
        _nav = nav;
    }

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? _selectedItem;

    // Fires automatically when SelectedItem changes
    partial void OnSelectedItemChanged(Item? value)
    {
        if (value is null) return;

        _nav.NavigateAsync<DetailPage>(new { Id = value.Id });

        // Clear selection so the same item can be re-selected later
        SelectedItem = null;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false;   // stops the spinner
        }
    }
}
```

**Key binding points:**

| XAML Property | ViewModel Property | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` | `Items` (ObservableCollection) | Drives the list |
| `SelectionMode="Single"` | — | Enables tap-to-select |
| `SelectedItem` | `SelectedItem` (two-way by default) | Triggers `OnSelectedItemChanged` → navigates |
| `RefreshView.Command` | `RefreshCommand` (generated by `[RelayCommand]`) | Pull-to-refresh calls API |
| `RefreshView.IsRefreshing` | `IsRefreshing` | Controls the refresh spinner |

**Important details:**
- **`RefreshView` wraps `CollectionView`** — not the other way around.
- Set `IsRefreshing = false` in a `finally` block so the spinner always stops.
- Reset `SelectedItem = null` after navigation so the user can tap the same row again.
- The `[ObservableProperty]` and `[RelayCommand]` attributes are from `CommunityToolkit.Mvvm` and generate all the boilerplate (`INotifyPropertyChanged`, `ICommand`, etc.).
